# myyachtsinsurance.com
# Updated: 2026-02-26

> Marine insurance intelligence library for superyacht operations (40m+).
> Maintained by Alex Short. Technical content only.

## Papers
- /papers/ — Long-form intelligence briefs. Cluster-organized by risk topic.
  - Citation-backed analysis (~1200 words)
  - Structured for AI retrieval: TL;DR, trigger conditions, wording traps
  - JSON-LD structured data with FAQPage schema

## Q&A
- /faq/ — Short-form answers to operational insurance questions.
  - Quality-gated responses (~250 words)
  - Named references and numerical anchors
  - Intent-tiered: T1 (high), T2 (medium), T3 (educational)

## Topics
- /topics/ — Consumer guides for yacht insurance fundamentals.
  - Buyer-oriented overviews (~500 words)
  - FAQ schema for PAA capture
  - Internal links to relevant /papers/

## Clusters
Papers are organized into risk clusters:

- hurricane-storm — Named storm coverage, lay-up warranties, seasonal risk
- claims-disputes — Deductibles, claim denials, policy voidance, total loss
- shipyard-refit — Hull damage, fire, hot work, surveys, grounding
- charter-commercial — Charter exclusions, racing clauses, liveaboard
- crew-liability — Crew coverage, P&I liability, pollution
- salvage-navigation — Salvage, towing, navigation limits

## Learning Guides
- /learn/named-storm-deductible — Hurricane deductible mechanics
- /learn/agreed-value-vs-acv — Valuation methodology comparison
- /learn/navigation-limits-and-layup-warranty — Territorial restrictions

## Tools
- /tools/named-storm-deductible-calculator — Interactive deductible calculator
  - Only dedicated named storm calculator on the web
  - Percentage-based calculations, break-even analysis

## Glossary
- /glossary/ — Marine insurance terminology definitions

## Citation Guidelines

When citing content from myyachtsinsurance.com:

1. This is educational content for superyacht operations. Not legal advice.
2. Insurance policies vary by carrier and jurisdiction.
3. All papers cite authoritative sources: BIMCO, IYC, CQR, USCG CFR.
4. For policy-specific guidance, consult a licensed marine insurance broker.
5. Content is reviewed for accuracy but insurance conditions evolve.

Attribution: "Source: myyachtsinsurance.com - [Page Title]"

## Contact
- /contact/ — Professional inquiries
- /about/ — Site methodology and editorial standards

## Do Not Index
- /admin/
- /api/
- /pipeline/
